code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Product failure with safety risks or complete dysfunction
    - Multiple defects across purchases/updates
    - Explicit "return/don't buy" recommendations
    - Negative updates overriding initial positivity

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failures despite some positives
    - Regret language ("waste of money", "misleading")
    - Requires constant workarounds for basic use
    - Comparative inferiority to alternatives

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Partial functionality requiring adjustments
    - "Does the job but..." phrasing without recommendation
    - Target audience restrictions without endorsement

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Single functional criticism with maintained recommendation
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only reason not 5 stars")
    - Requires preprocessing/break-in period for full function
    - Short positive statements without enthusiastic language
    - Resolved issues with lingering mention (e.g. "had smell but...")

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Multiple complementary aspects praised (≥3 features)
    - Practical benefits outweigh stated limitations
    - Non-functional criticism with core functionality praise
    - Long-term validation (≥6 months) with sustained performance
    - "Worth every penny" declarations with demonstrated utility
    - Meets specialized needs exceptionally ("perfect for [use case]")

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Multiple purchase failures →1 even if initial experience positive
    - "Would be 5 stars but [functional issue]" →4
    - "Still 5 stars despite [non-functional quirk]" →5
    - Competitive pricing praise + functional flaw →4
    - Temporary issues resolved enthusiastically →5
    - Inherent material properties ≠ product flaws
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1 (5→4 Fix):
Review: "So far this blender works well. Makes great smoothies and looks sharp in red."
<Reasoning>Multiple positive aspects (functionality, design) without criticisms. Warrants 5 despite tentative "so far".</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2 (4→5 Fix):
Review: "The towels are 100% cotton and measure 27x55 inches. Dobby weave gives good absorbency after 2 washes."
<Reasoning>Functional praise despite need for break-in period. No explicit criticism →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3 (Gold 4):
Review: "Love the design but fabric sags when full. Perfect for lightweight items."
<Reasoning>Clear recommendation with functional limitation. Maintained positivity →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 4 (5-Star Benchmark):
Review: "Silent operation, perfect size, durable build. Minor lid quirk irrelevant."
<Reasoning>Multiple strengths with dismissed drawback. Comprehensive satisfaction →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 5 (Borderline 4):
Review: "EDIT: After washing, towels work well but initial absorbency poor."
<Reasoning>Resolved issue still mentioned as functional drawback →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 6 (1-Star Critical):
Review: "UPDATE: Failed twice within 6 months - dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multiple safety-related failures override any positives →1.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 7 (3-Star Compromise):
Review: "Gets job done with frequent adjustments. Okay for price."
<Reasoning>Adequate performance with usability compromises →3.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 8 (5-Star Update):
Review: "A year later - still perfect! Minor noise irrelevant."
<Reasoning>Long-term validation outweighs trivial drawback →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9 (2-Star Regret):
Review: "Beautiful but cracks under normal use. Wouldn't recommend."
<Reasoning>Core failure despite aesthetic appeal →2.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 10 (5-Star Material):
Review: "Cotton wrinkles but absorbs better than competitors. Worth it!"
<Reasoning>Material property outweighed by core functionality →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>
</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (31 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 12
  gold 5 -> 4: 9
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 3 -> 2: 2

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "Not much to say. They work!"
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "These not only look great, but they are actually usable. I've bought napkins for my place settings in the past that looks so nice, then I realized they were completely nonabsorbent. So while they looked good, they were pretty much worthless as an act..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "These mats are fine if you like to switch them out a lot. If you refer to have a very fresh and new bathmat for the bathroom when guests are staying for a visit, then these have a fun knobbly like texture to them and are cheap enough to pick up for s..."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [2, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "On my computer screen the color for the 'cool brown' bath mat is accurate.  It's a faded sort of brown with lots of gray tint to it.  Our upstairs bath is in browns -- golden-hue brown and tile brown-- and it fits in.

What you get is a cool-brown no..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 31 errors, 21 borderline out of 80 examples."}
